text.title: java-spring-async Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for java-spring-async as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Using the default executor without a custom `ThreadPoolTaskExecutor` bean.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Spring's default `SimpleAsyncTaskExecutor` creates a new thread per call with no pooling. OOM and thread explosion on high load. Default executor changes between Boot versions; behavior is unpredictable.

failure-mode.description: Mixing `@Async` methods on the same bean without explicit executor names.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: All methods use the default executor. Slow email tasks starve fast cache-warm tasks. Separate executors allow independent tuning.

failure-mode.description: `@Async` method with `void` return type and no exception handler.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Exceptions are swallowed completely. Silent failures. Caller cannot observe or retry.

failure-mode.description: Caller does not `.get()` or `.thenAccept()` on the returned `CompletableFuture`.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Fire-and-forget without visibility. If the async work fails, no one knows. Silent corruption.

failure-mode.description: Async method that calls `SecurityContextHolder.getContext()` or uses `@Transactional` without a `TaskDecorator`.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: SecurityContext is null in the new thread. `@Transactional` requires the transaction context (from Spring's `TransactionContextHolder`), also lost. Method fails with cryptic NullPointerException or `RuntimeException: not transactional`.

failure-mode.description: Wrapping an `@Async` method in `@Transactional` expecting the async work to be part of the transaction.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The async method runs in a new thread with a new transaction (or no transaction, depending on propagation). Partial failure leaves inconsistent state. Rollback is not coordinated.

failure-mode.description: Using `Thread.sleep()` in a test to wait for an async task.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Flaky on CI under load. Use `Awaitility.await().atMost(...)` instead for deterministic completion detection.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Thread pools are for IO-bound work. CPU-bound parallelism requires `ForkJoinPool` or Project Reactor. `@Async` will add overhead and contention.
